title: Introduction
description: NOC 64300 – Maîtres d'hôtel and hosts/hostesses | GTR Immigration NOC 64300 (Maîtres d'hôtel and hosts/hostesses) represents the face of Canada's premium dining and hospitality sectors. These TEER 4 professionals are responsible for the first impression of an establishment, managing guest flow, reservations, and front-of-house coordination. In Canada's high-growth tourism market, a skilled Maître d'hôtel is vital to the success of fine-dining restaurants and hotels. For immigration, this role is a staple for BC PNP and Atlantic Canada pathways, offering a fast-track to PR for those with exceptional communication and organizational skills.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level4Major Group64 – Sales and service representativesSub-Major Group643 – Occupations in travel and hospitalityMinor Group6430 – Occupations in travel and hospitalityNOC Code64300

duties: Receive and record guests' reservations and assign tables.
Greet guests at the entrance of the dining room or restaurant and escort them to their tables.
Coordinate the activities of food and beverage servers and other kitchen staff.
Inspect dining rooms, guest areas, and service stations to ensure standards are met.
Handle guest complaints and ensure a high level of customer satisfaction.
employment_requirements: Completion of secondary school is usually required.
Several years of experience in the food service or hospitality industry are typically required.
Maîtres d'hôtel usually require experience as a food and beverage server.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable. Projections indicate a steady increase in job openings as the tourism sector continues to recover and expand its premium service offerings.
labour_market_demand: The demand is currently Very Good, particularly in provinces like British Columbia and Ontario. As Canada's luxury dining scene expands, establishments are facing a shortage of experienced front-of-house leaders. Bilingual (English/French) hosts and Maîtres d'hôtel are in high demand in Quebec and major international hubs like Ottawa and Toronto.
Hospitality Growth: Work in world-class restaurants that are gaining Michelin recognition in Canada.
Path to Management: Clear progression to Restaurant Manager (NOC 60030).
Workplace Culture: Professional environments that value interpersonal excellence and organizational skill.
pathways: BC PNP Skills Immigration: Specialized hospitality draws frequently target this NOC.
Atlantic Immigration Program (AIP): A high-conversion pathway for candidates with job offers.
Rural and Northern Immigration Pilot (RNIP): Addressing hospitality needs in participating regions.
